# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_rest_info (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fields` (String) Request specific fields from subset. Recommended - '<list of fields>' to return specified fields, only one subset will be allowed. Discouraged - '*' to return all the fields, one or more subsets are allowed. This option can be used for discovery, but is discouraged in production. Stongly discouraged - '**' to return all the fields, one or more subsets are allowed. This option can put an extra load on the system and should not be used in production. Limited - '' to return default fields, generally the properties that uniquely identify the record (keys). Other data is not returned by default and need to be explicitly called for using the field name or *. If the option is not present, return default fields for that API (see '' above).
- `gather_subset` (String) When supplied, this argument will restrict the information collected to a given subset. Either the REST API or the ZAPI info name can be given. Possible values for this argument include application/applications or application_info application/consistency-groups application/consistency-groups/metrics **Requires the owning_resource to be set** application/consistency-groups/snapshots **Requires the owning_resource to be set** application/templates or application_template_info cloud/targets or cloud_targets_info cluster cluster/chassis or REST APIs are supported with ONTAP 9.6 onwards.
- `hal_linking` (Boolean) if false, HAL-encoded links are disabled in the REST calls.
- `ignore_api_errors` (String) List of substrings. If a substring is contained in an error message when fetching a subset, the module does not fail and the error is reported in the subset.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `max_records` (Number) Maximum number of records returned in a single call.
- `owning_resource` (String) Some resources cannot be accessed directly.  You need to select them based on the owner or parent.  For instance, volume for a snapshot. The following subsets require an owning resource, and the following suboptions when uuid is not present. <application/consistency-groups/metrics> **cg_name** is the consistency group name, **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the consistency group. <application/consistency-groups/snapshots> **cg_name** is the consistency group name, **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the consistency group. <storage/volumes/snapshots>  **volume_name** is the volume name, **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the volume. <protocols/nfs/export-policies/rules> **policy_name** is the name of the policy, **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the policy, **rule_index** is the rule index. <protocols/vscan/on-access-policies> **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the vscan. <protocols/vscan/on-demand-policies> **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the vscan. <protocols/vscan/scanner-pools> **svm_name** is the owning vserver name for the vscan.
- `parameters` (String) Allows for any rest option to be passed in
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) deprecated as of 21.1.0. this option was ignored and continues to be ignored.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_python_keys` (Boolean) If true, */* in the returned dictionary keys are translated to *_*. It makes it possible to use a . notation when processing the output. For instance *ontap_info["svm/svms"]* can be accessed as *ontap_info.svm_svms*.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
